popularStory.headline: My Maternal 17th. Scottish Grandfather, Sir William de Keith, 12th. Marischal of Scotland

Born: 1315 in Dunottar Castle, Aberdeenshire, Scotland
Married: before 3 May 1351, in Scotland to Lady Margaret Fraser, of Touch fraser

zh.lifeSummary: Margaret Fraser是John Fraser和Mary Bisset的女兒，於1328年出生在英國蘇格蘭費伏。她和Laird Robert Stewart of Shambothy, Innermeath and Durisdeer至少有1個女兒。她去世於1410年在英國蘇格蘭金加迪爾斯同哈文享年82歲，埋葬在英國蘇格蘭阿爾迪史爾。

nameMeanings.name: Fraser
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Scottish: apparently a nickname from Anglo-Norman French fraser(e), fresere ‘strawberry plant’. Fresel and Frisel, from Anglo-Norman French fresel ‘strawberry’, are early variants of the Fraser surname, the modern Gaelicized form of which is Friseal. See Frizzell . The crest on the Scottish family's coat of arms takes the form of a strawberry plant, but its antiquity is unknown. Claims of a habitational derivation, in particular from a place called la Frézelière in Anjou (France), are attractive but they lack verifiable evidence.
Americanized form of one or more similar (like-sounding) Jewish surnames.

History: There are two clans Fraser in Scotland, with common ancestry going back to Simon Fraser of Keith in East Lothian, who lived in the 12th century. One of these clans has its seat at Philorth Castle (subsequently re-named Cairnbulg) on the northeast coast of Scotland. Sir Alexander Fraser, 8th laird of Philorth (c. 1536–1623) converted the fishing village of Faithlie into the burgh of Fraserburgh in the 1590s. The other clan is Fraser of Lovat, associated mainly with the city of Inverness are. They are descended from Simon Fraser, a younger son, who lived in the early 14th century. In Gaelic, the head of Clan Fraser of Lovat is known as Mac Shimi ‘son of Simon’.

variants.name: Frayser
meanings: Probably an altered form of   Fraser  .

variants.name: Frasier

variants.name: Frazer
meanings: Variant of   Fraser  , associated chiefly with northern Ireland.

variants.name: Frashier

variants.name: Frizzell
meanings: English and Irish: nickname from Old French fresel, frisel ‘lace, ribbon, braid, brooch, frill, ruff’, for someone with an ornate style of dress, or ‘object of little value’ (a diminutive of frese, of ancient Germanic origin).
Scottish: from Anglo-Norman French fresel ‘strawberry’, an alternative form of Fraser .

variants.name: Frazier
meanings: English and Scottish: variant of   Fraser  .

variants.name: Frasher
meanings: Scottish and northern Irish: variant of   Fraser  .
Possibly an altered form of German Fratzscher (see Frutchey ).

nameMeanings.name: Margaret
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: An extremely common given name from the Middle Ages onwards, derived via Old French Marguerite and Latin Margarita from Greek Margarītēs, from margaron ‘pearl’, a word ultimately of Hebrew origin. The name was always understood to mean ‘pearl’ throughout the Middle Ages. The first St Margaret was martyred at Antioch in Pisidia during the persecution instigated by the Emperor Diocletian in the early 4th century. However, there is some doubt about her name, as the same saint is venerated in the Orthodox Church as Marina . There were several other saintly bearers of the name, including St Margaret of Scotland ( d. 1093 ), wife of King Malcolm Canmore and daughter of Edmund Ironside of England. It was also the name of the wife of Henry VI of England, Margaret of Anjou ( 1430–82 ), and of Margaret Tudor ( 1489–1541 ), sister of Henry VIII , who married James IV of Scotland and ruled as regent there after his death. See also Margery , Marjorie .

variants.name: Margareta
meanings: From the Latin form of Greek Margarītēs. See Margaret .

variants.name: Margaretta
meanings: An extremely common given name from the Middle Ages onwards, derived via Old French Marguerite and Latin Margarita from Greek Margarītēs, from margaron ‘pearl’, a word ultimately of Hebrew origin. The name was always understood to mean ‘pearl’ throughout the Middle Ages. The first St Margaret was martyred at Antioch in Pisidia during the persecution instigated by the Emperor Diocletian in the early 4th century. However, there is some doubt about her name, as the same saint is venerated in the Orthodox Church as Marina . There were several other saintly bearers of the name, including St Margaret of Scotland ( d. 1093 ), wife of King Malcolm Canmore and daughter of Edmund Ironside of England. It was also the name of the wife of Henry VI of England, Margaret of Anjou ( 1430–82 ), and of Margaret Tudor ( 1489–1541 ), sister of Henry VIII , who married James IV of Scotland and ruled as regent there after his death. See also Margery , Marjorie .

en.lifeSummary: Margaret Fraser was born in 1328, in Fife, Scotland, United Kingdom as the daughter of John Fraser and Mary Bisset. She had at least 1 daughter with Laird Robert Stewart of Shambothy, Innermeath and Durisdeer. She died in 1410, in Stonehaven, Kincardineshire, Scotland, United Kingdom, at the age of 82, and was buried in Aberdeenshire, Scotland, United Kingdom.

fr.lifeSummary: Margaret Fraser est née en 1328, à Fife, Écosse, Royaume-Uni, fille de John Fraser et Mary Bisset. Elle a eu au moins 1 fille avec Laird Robert Stewart of Shambothy, Innermeath and Durisdeer. Elle est décédée en 1410, à Stonehaven, Kincardineshire, Écosse, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 82 ans, et a été enterrée à Aberdeenshire, Écosse, Royaume-Uni.

de.lifeSummary: Margaret Fraser wurde 1328 in Fife, Schottland, United Kingdom, als Tochter von John Fraser und Mary Bisset geboren. Sie hatte mit Laird Robert Stewart of Shambothy, Innermeath and Durisdeer mindestens 1 Tochter. Sie starb 1410 im Alter von 82 Jahren in Stonehaven, Kincardineshire, Schottland, United Kingdom und wurde in Aberdeenshire, Schottland, United Kingdom begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Margaret Fraser è nata il nel 1328 nel Fife, Scozia, Regno Unito come figlia di John Fraser e Mary Bisset. Lei avuto almeno 1 figlia con Laird Robert Stewart of Shambothy, Innermeath and Durisdeer. Morì nel 1410, nel Stonehaven, Kincardineshire, Scozia, Regno Unito, all'età di 82 anni, e fu sepolta a Aberdeenshire, Scozia, Regno Unito.

ja.lifeSummary: Margaret Fraserは1328年にイギリススコットランドフィフでJohn FraserとMary Bissetの娘として生まれた。彼女はLaird Robert Stewart of Shambothy, Innermeath and Durisdeerとの間に少なくとも1人の娘をもうけた。彼女は1410年にイギリススコットランドキンカーディンエシュアイアストーネヘイベンで82歳で死去し，イギリススコットランドアバーディーンシュアイアにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Margaret Fraser(은)는 1328년 영국 스코틀랜드 피프에서 John Fraser(와)과 Mary Bisset의 딸로 태어났다. 그녀는 Laird Robert Stewart of Shambothy, Innermeath and Durisdeer(와)과 적어도 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1410년 영국 스코틀랜드 킨카딘실르 스톤하븐에서 82세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 스코틀랜드 압르디실르에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Margaret Fraser nasceu em 1328, em Fife, Escócia, United Kingdom filha de John Fraser e Mary Bisset. Ela teve pelo menos 1 filha com Laird Robert Stewart of Shambothy, Innermeath and Durisdeer. Ela faleceu em 1410, em Stonehaven, Kincardineshire, Escócia, United Kingdom, com 82 anos, e foi sepultada em Aberdeenshire, Escócia, United Kingdom.

ru.lifeSummary: Margaret Fraser родилась в 1328 г. в местности фиф, Шотландия, Великобритания. Её родителей звали John Fraser и Mary Bisset. У неё была по крайней мере 1 дочь от Laird Robert Stewart of Shambothy, Innermeath and Durisdeer. Она умерла в 1410 г. в местности стоунэавен, кинкардаинешир, Шотландия, Великобритания в возрасте 82 лет и была похоронена в местности абирдиншир, Шотландия, Великобритания.

es.lifeSummary: Margaret Fraser nació en 1328, en Fife, Escocia, Reino Unido como hija de John Fraser y Mary Bisset. Tuvo por lo menos 1 hija con Laird Robert Stewart of Shambothy, Innermeath and Durisdeer. Murió en 1410, en Stonehaven, Kincardineshire, Escocia, Reino Unido, a la edad de 82 años, y fue sepultada en Aberdeenshire, Escocia, Reino Unido.
